IDTemplate.title: IDTemplate
IDTemplate.description: Defines the structure, available input fields, and QR code configuration for a specific type of identification card (e.g., Student ID, Employee Badge).

id.description: Unique identifier for the IDTemplate entity.

name.description: Descriptive name of the ID template (e.g., 'Student ID Card', 'Company Employee Badge').

description.description: A brief explanation of the template's purpose and the type of information it's designed to hold.

availableFieldNames.description: A list of programmatic names for fields that can be used with this template (e.g., 'fullName', 'dateOfBirth', 'studentId'). These names correspond to keys expected in the 'cardData' of a 'GeneratedID'.

qrCodeFieldNames.description: A list of programmatic names for fields whose values should be concatenated and included in the generated QR code for IDs based on this template.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the ID template was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the ID template was last updated.

GeneratedID.title: GeneratedID
GeneratedID.description: Represents a single instance of a user-generated identification card, based on a specific template and containing individual information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GeneratedID entity.

templateId.description: Reference to the IDTemplate entity that defines the structure and type of this ID. (Relationship: IDTemplate 1:N GeneratedID)

creatorUserId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the user who created this ID. (Assumes an external authentication system).

cardData.description: A JSON string containing the key-value pairs of all user-inputted information for this specific ID (e.g., '{\"fullName\": \"Alice Smith\", \"dateOfBirth\": \"1990-01-01\", \"studentId\": \"S12345\"}'). The keys correspond to 'availableFieldNames' in the associated IDTemplate.

photoUrl.description: URL or path to the uploaded and AI-verified photo associated with this ID.

qrCodeContent.description: The actual string content that is encoded into the QR code for this ID.

status.description: The current status of the generated ID (e.g., 'draft', 'finalized', 'archived').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this ID was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this ID was last updated.

Logement.title: Logement
Logement.description: Represents a short-term rental apartment listing.

title.description: The title of the apartment listing.

description.description: A detailed description of the apartment.

address.description: The physical address of the apartment.

pricePerNight.description: The price per night in USD.

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs for the apartment images.

ownerId.description: The UID of the user who owns this listing (the admin).

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the listing was created.

TransportSubscription.title: Transport Subscription
TransportSubscription.description: Represents a user's subscription to an organized transport service, including details about the service, payment, and vehicle.

userId.description: The UID of the subscribing user.

address.description: User's full street address of residence.

workplace.description: User's place of work (company name or area).

transportType.description: Type of transport service subscribed to.

departureTime.description: Preferred departure time from home (HH:MM format).

returnTime.description: Preferred return time from work (HH:MM format).

status.description: The current status of the subscription.

price.description: The monthly subscription price in USD, set by the admin.

rejectionReason.description: Reason for rejection, if status is 'rejected'.

subscriptionDate.description: Timestamp when the subscription becomes active (after payment).

carType.description: The type/model of the assigned vehicle.

carColor.description: The color of the assigned vehicle.

licensePlate.description: The license plate of the assigned vehicle.

driverName.description: The name of the assigned driver.

driverPhone.description: The phone number of the assigned driver.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the subscription request was made.

definition.description: Collection storing definitions of various ID card templates. Accessible for reading by all authenticated users, and for writing only by administrators. No denormalized authorization fields needed beyond global read/admin write.

params.name: templateId
params.description: Unique identifier for the ID template.

definition.description: Subcollection storing individual ID cards generated by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'creatorUserId' field for authorization independence, matching the path's '{userId}'.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier of the user who owns these generated IDs.

params.name: generatedId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific generated ID card.

definition.description: Collection used for 'Existence over Content' based access control, indicating users with administrative privileges. Only the document ID is relevant for authorization checks in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier of a user with administrative privileges.

definition.description: Collection storing apartment listings. Publicly readable, but only writable by administrators.

params.name: logementId
params.description: Unique identifier for the apartment listing.

definition.description: Stores a user's subscription to a transport service. This data is private and only accessible by the user who created it, or by an administrator.

params.name: userId
params.description: Unique identifier of the user who owns this subscription.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific transport subscription.

firestore.reasoning:
The proposed Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and scalable queries. 

1.  **ID Templates (`/id_templates/{templateId}`):** This top-level collection stores definitions of various ID card templates. It's designed for global read access by all authenticated users, allowing them to browse and select templates. Write access is restricted to administrators, managed via a dedicated `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection. This structure achieves **Authorization Independence** as access to templates does not depend on any parent document. It supports **QAPs** by allowing simple `list` operations for all authenticated users to view available templates, without needing client-side filtering or complex security rules.

2.  **Generated IDs (`/users/{userId}/generated_ids/{generatedId}`):** This subcollection is nested under a user-specific path (`/users/{userId}`). Each document here represents a unique ID card generated by a specific user. This follows the **Path-Based Ownership** and **Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data** principles, making the owner (`creatorUserId`) explicit both in the document's content and its path. Crucially, the `creatorUserId` field within the `GeneratedID` document **denormalizes** the authorization context, directly matching the `{userId}` wildcard in the path. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to a parent `user` document to determine ownership, thereby ensuring **Authorization Independence**. This structure also supports **QAPs** effectively: a user can only list their own generated IDs by querying their specific subcollection (`/users/currentUserId/generated_ids`), making the `list` operation inherently secure and efficient. The `status` field (e.g., 'draft', 'finalized') within the `GeneratedID` document provides **Explicit State Modeling**, allowing internal workflow changes without altering the security posture of the collection.

3.  **Admin Roles (`/roles_admin/{userId}`):** This is a top-level collection used for **Global Roles (DBAC)**, specifically employing the **Existence over Content** pattern. An admin user's UID will exist as a document ID in this collection. This allows Firestore Security Rules to check for administrative privileges using a simple `exists()` call (e.g., `allow write: if exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`) without requiring `get()` calls, further reinforcing **Authorization Independence** and simplifying rule logic. This collection is not intended for direct app interaction but solely for rules evaluation.

A new subcollection `transport_subscriptions` is added under `/users/{userId}` to store user-specific transport service requests, maintaining the Path-Based Ownership pattern for privacy and security.
